--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-03-21) ---
[2018-03-21 22:20] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): One more set to talk about
[2018-03-21 22:20] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): is scorchilla viable?
[2018-03-21 22:20] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): or is it better to go like grimbotch lancetooth
[2018-03-21 22:20] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): hellhound isn't gonna see much play here obv
[2018-03-21 22:21] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): the question is mostly just whether you go scorchilla or not, I suppose
[2018-03-21 22:23] mrguy888: Why will hound obviously see no play?
[2018-03-21 22:24] mqp: i think any unit can play in this set
[2018-03-21 22:24] mrguy888: Seems like every unit is relevant. Tatsu probably can't make it out in normal play but every other unit is relevant.
[2018-03-21 22:25] 307th: i think scorchilla's a bit too slow here
[2018-03-21 22:25] .bky_1556 (bky): Tatsu  might doom some walls in a few lines.
[2018-03-21 22:26] mqp: i think the highest power level things are steelforge, hellhound, lancetooth, frostbite, and grimbotch, so i am excited to build an economy to buy those as efficiently as possible
[2018-03-21 22:26] mrguy888: P2 scorches just as first attackers seems fine. da seems too fast for p2
[2018-03-21 22:26] mqp: there are so many kinds that your moves are super flexible so i don't know what the "ideal" economy size for that stuff is
[2018-03-21 22:26] .bky_1556 (bky): The biggest problem with Tatsu is that it's competing with Frostbite?
[2018-03-21 22:26] mrguy888: Biggest problem with Tastu is that you need to have 2 Animuss to buy it.
[2018-03-21 22:27] .bky_1556 (bky): Between Steelforge, Frostbite, Scorch and Hellhound it's a high tech-to-gold set.
[2018-03-21 22:31] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): yeah, lots of cheap things
[2018-03-21 22:32] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): definetly the steelforge imo
[2018-03-21 22:32] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): but does p1 start natural blastforge or natural animus?
[2018-03-21 22:32] themightyflango (non alpha pleb): I guess it kinda depends on p2
[2018-03-21 22:40] mqp: p1 i start animus. reason: i don't want to have a single blue so soon to spend on anything but lancetooth
[2018-03-21 22:49] gadget246: how do you evaluate when a set is NOT a breachproof set
because at the moment if I see a good green unit in the random set and nothing else sticks out at me for attacks I just go breachproof every time, which I feel like might be suboptimal
[2018-03-21 22:49] gadget246: it works for now because i'm just starting casual matches so my opponents aren't any better lol
[2018-03-21 22:50] 307th: so, there's 'pure' breachproof and semi breachproof
[2018-03-21 22:50] 307th: pure breachproof is when you can convert all of your Drones into a higher health unit
[2018-03-21 22:51] 307th: so, it's only possible when there's something in the random set that lets you do that - Trinity Drone, Thorium Dynamo, or Venge Cannon
[2018-03-21 22:51] 307th: semi breachproof is when you just make breachproof attackers but don't convert your Drones, it tends to be less strong
[2018-03-21 22:52] 307th: you usually don't go for semi breachproof straight away - instead you play standard for a while (defending and absorbing) and transition into semi breachproof later when attack gets so high that it isn't worth defending
[2018-03-21 22:52] 307th: even that type of semi breachproof is still fairly rare, since usually the first player to allow a breahc just loses
[2018-03-21 22:53] 307th: even when pure breachproof is possible, it's still usually not correct
[2018-03-21 22:53] 307th: you want to look at how strong the breachproof strategy is, and also at how strong the standard, not-breachproof strategy is
[2018-03-21 22:54] 307th: things that make the non-breachproof  strategy stronger is stuff like good absorber, good soak, good (non-breachproof) attackers
[2018-03-21 22:55] 307th: trinity drone isn't actually that good for breachproof, just because it tends to be really difficult to efficiently convert all of your regular drones into trinity drones
[2018-03-21 22:56] 307th: with venge cannon because it turns your drones into attackers, it doesn't have much synergy with most other green units, and by itself it's quite weak
[2018-03-21 22:56] 307th: so i'd recommend only going for venge cannon all-ins (where you store up a bunch of green and then turn all your drones into venge cannons) when cluster bolt is in the set
[2018-03-21 22:57] 307th: cluster bolt actually has really good synergy with venge cannon, since it lets you efficiently spend the green from your conduits post all-in
[2018-03-21 22:58] 307th: there will be other times when venge cannon all-ins are good even when cluster bolt isn't in the set, but they'll be rare - the main example i can think of is using it to counter a low or mid econ red strategy - venge all-ins are good vs. those since red can't make defense efficiently
[2018-03-21 22:59] 307th: thorium dynamo is definitely the best breachproof-enabler at the moment
[2018-03-21 22:59] 307th: if there's thorium dynamo and a decent breachproof attacker in the set, then you know the breachproof strategy is at least moderately strong
[2018-03-21 22:59] .bky_1556 (bky): Trinity is good enough for P2's high econ non-breachproof strategies.
[2018-03-21 22:59] 307th: so if the standard strategy isn't that good (no absorber, not good soak) then you can try it out
[2018-03-21 23:00] 307th: wild drone can also support some semi breachproof strategies
[2018-03-21 23:00] 307th: since it tends to lead to situations where you don't want to defend
[2018-03-21 23:00] 307th: and it's pretty bulky
[2018-03-21 23:01] .bky_1556 (bky): Green's better long term attackers tend to push away from breachproof. For example, Mahar is used for its value as a blocker, and Tantalum is best when it can be clicked all the way down to 1.
[2018-03-21 23:01] gadget246: awesome! thank you.
what if there's great absorb but no good attackers in the set and there's iso chronus available? would semi breachproof be considerable?
[2018-03-21 23:01] 307th: no, if there's great absorb you really want to defend
[2018-03-21 23:02] .bky_1556 (bky): Good absorb means non-breachproof is higher value
[2018-03-21 23:02] 307th: i think it's generally a good idea to try out breachproof a bit too much early on though
[2018-03-21 23:02] 307th: and take some losses from doing it when it's not good

--- Thread (7 messages, 2018-03-21) ---

[2018-03-22 17:48] drevoed: What counters breach-proof? Like, when I know that second player is going DA turn 2, I can preemptively counter him with DB on my turn 2. But when I know opponent will go full breach-proof, I always end up trying to do my own breach-proof better than he can.
[2018-03-22 17:49] .bky_1556 (bky): If there's good absorb, breach-proof doesn't get to use it.
[2018-03-22 17:49] drevoed: If there is good absorb, I won't know for sure they will be going breach-proof.
[2018-03-22 17:50] masn6811 (Masne98888): Do note that players can be flexible.
[2018-03-22 17:50] masn6811 (Masne98888): For example, in BSO: DD..DD/DD..DA is a win for p2
[2018-03-22 17:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): DD..DD/DB..DA is a win for p1
[2018-03-22 17:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): But against 1. DD 2. DB, p2 can win with 2. ..DDC
[2018-03-22 17:51] drevoed: Naturally, so I'm forcing him not to go da
[2018-03-22 17:54] drevoed: Like I recently had this set, where I knew for sure we were both going breach-proof from the start. But I never have went for anything else, other than outplaying them at their own game, when breach-proof is a good option.
[2018-03-22 17:54] p0lari (Polari): is 2.db even good against an anticipated 2.da if it's an actual pool with strong red units instead of BSO?
[2018-03-22 17:54] p0lari (Polari): if I had to pick a side there I'd rather be p2
[2018-03-22 17:55] extratricky: it depends on the set, but usually in breachproof vs non-breachproof the breachproof player eventually loses all of their economy while the non-breachproof player is trying to defend, so things that make the game longer favors the non-breachproof player. In particular good absorb (like energy matrix) and good soak (like hannibull) are very good against breachproof
[2018-03-22 17:56] .bky_1556 (bky): the only time I like 2.db vs a turn 1 DD is if I anticipate DA and ALSO have access to Oxide Mixer for DD1 if my opponent DDC's.
[2018-03-22 17:56] extratricky: also a lot of times breachproof starts out absorbing for a little bit, so I tend to focus on the soak more than the absorb
[2018-03-22 18:02] drevoed: How do you determine when it's the time to kill their breach-proof economy over units like militia?
[2018-03-22 18:02] extratricky: if you always kill economy first you'll be doing it right 90%+ of the time
[2018-03-22 18:03] steel0229e (Steel): the only times you kill their units is when they are almost out of supply of everything
[2018-03-22 18:05] drevoed: Yeah, so, about that, I thought he was out of green supply, then we started making steelsplitters. Surely, I thought, it's good to kill 3 steelsplitters over Dynamo, we will run out of them soon anyway. And then we began making red units just for the damage, so I felt forced to kill the 1hp tarsiers that were running out soon anyway.
[2018-03-22 18:05] supergreyman (Greyman): Or if you can OHKO key unit like Zemora.
[2018-03-22 18:05] wilson0862 (Wilson): Tbh i still have no idea why either of you went breachproof in that set, @Drevoed

messages.author: drevoed
messages.content: What counters breach-proof? Like, when I know that second player is going DA turn 2, I can preemptively counter him with DB on my turn 2. But when I know opponent will go full breach-proof, I always end up trying to do my own breach-proof better than he can.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: If there's good absorb, breach-proof doesn't get to use it.

messages.author: drevoed
messages.content: If there is good absorb, I won't know for sure they will be going breach-proof.

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: Do note that players can be flexible.

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: For example, in BSO: DD..DD/DD..DA is a win for p2

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: DD..DD/DB..DA is a win for p1

messages.author: masn6811
messages.content: But against 1. DD 2. DB, p2 can win with 2. ..DDC

messages.author: drevoed
messages.content: Naturally, so I'm forcing him not to go da

messages.author: drevoed
messages.content: Like I recently had this set, where I knew for sure we were both going breach-proof from the start. But I never have went for anything else, other than outplaying them at their own game, when breach-proof is a good option.

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: is 2.db even good against an anticipated 2.da if it's an actual pool with strong red units instead of BSO?

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: if I had to pick a side there I'd rather be p2

messages.author: extratricky
messages.content: it depends on the set, but usually in breachproof vs non-breachproof the breachproof player eventually loses all of their economy while the non-breachproof player is trying to defend, so things that make the game longer favors the non-breachproof player. In particular good absorb (like energy matrix) and good soak (like hannibull) are very good against breachproof

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: the only time I like 2.db vs a turn 1 DD is if I anticipate DA and ALSO have access to Oxide Mixer for DD1 if my opponent DDC's.

messages.author: extratricky
messages.content: also a lot of times breachproof starts out absorbing for a little bit, so I tend to focus on the soak more than the absorb

messages.author: drevoed
messages.content: How do you determine when it's the time to kill their breach-proof economy over units like militia?

messages.author: extratricky
messages.content: if you always kill economy first you'll be doing it right 90%+ of the time

messages.author: steel0229e
messages.content: the only times you kill their units is when they are almost out of supply of everything

messages.content: Yeah, so, about that, I thought he was out of green supply, then we started making steelsplitters. Surely, I thought, it's good to kill 3 steelsplitters over Dynamo, we will run out of them soon anyway.
